rulings.Case Title: TAROUB H RUSNAK VS. JANICE SALAMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Subpoena To City Nation Bank
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 22, PLAINTIFF TAROUB RUSNAK's Motion To Enforce Subpoena To City National Bank. Denied. This is a post-judgment matter. The third-party business records subpoena served on City National Bank is not one of the procedures provided in CCP §§ 708.010 through 709.030 to pursue discovery in connection with collection of a judgment. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GIANCARLO MARANGHI VS. BENNY CHETCUTI JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Consolidate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GIANCARLO MARANGHI's Motion To Consolidate. Denied. Plaintiffs have not shown that the pending cases involve common question of law or fact sufficient to warrant consolidation under CCP § 1048(a), or that consolidating the cases would avoid unnecessary costs or delay. Further, the cases were filed at different times and are at varying stages of discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHARMAINE FELGUTH VS. DAVID WILLIAMS EPSTEIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Pltf'S Expert Witness List
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff CHARMAINE FELGUTH'S Motion to Augment Plaintiff's Expert Witness List Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff CHARMAINE FELGUTH's Motion to Augment Witness List is GRANTED. The Court finds the conditions specified in C.C.P. § 2034.620 have been met. The Court requests that the parties meet and confer concerning a date certain for the deposition of Dr. Conor O'Neill, such that the Order of the Court may specify such date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Deposition Of Def John W. Simonse Produce More Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiffs MARCIA GIUSTI, ANTHONY T. VLAHIOTIS DDS ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF, YANNIS VLAHOS DDS ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND AS, DONALD KINOSIAN DDS'S Motion to Compel Further Deposition of Defendant John W. Simonse; Produce More Documents; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs MARCIA GIUSTI, et al.'s Motion to Compel Further Deposition of Defendant JOHN W. SIMONSE, Etc. is DENIED. Plaintiffs' Notice does not state the procedural grounds for the motion; Plaintiffs request for additional documents does not comply with C.C.P. § 2025.450(b)(1); and Plaintiffs have not met the requirements specified in C.C.P. § 2025.480. Plaintiff's Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLEY WIENKE, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF THE VS. CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC., A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jpmorgan Chase Bank, N.A.'S Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 23, Defendant Jpmorgan Chase Bank, N.A.'S Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint. Sustained in part. As to the first cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff was granted leave to amend her Second Amended Complaint to allege Defendant Chase unequivocally failed to contact Plaintiff prior to recording the Notice of Default and Plaintiff did not do so. As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff was granted leave to amend to allege what portion of the contract was breached and Plaintiff failed to do so. As to the third, fourth and fifth causes of action the demurrer is overruled. As to the seventh cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action was foreclosed by Gomes v. Countrywide Home Loans, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal. App. 4th 1149, 1152 n. 7, cert. denied 132 S.Ct. 419 (2011). As to the eighth, ninth and tenth causes of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to plead ultimate facts supporting the causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC., VS. PHOENIX RABBIT, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 6: Defendant PHOENIX RABBIT, LLC'S Motion for Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant PHOENIX RABBIT, LLC's Motion for Terminating Sanctions is GRANTED against Plaintiff TELEGRAPH HILL PROPERTIES, INC. for failure to comply with the Court's Order filed May 13, 2013, which required the personal appearance of WB Coyle at his deposition on June 4, 2013. Pursuant to C.C.P. § 2023.030(d)(2), an order shall issue that further proceedings in the instant matter are stayed until the May 13th discovery Order is obeyed, to wit: WB Coyle appears personally for his deposition, and such deposition is completed on or before a date certain to be submitted to the Court. Defendant's Request for Sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $6,985. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TORO MEDIA LLC, et al VS. ASPIRE ROCK LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTs ASPIRE ROCK LLC and EASY.LY INC.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion to vacate the defaults of Aspire Rock, LLC and Easy.Ly Inc. is granted. Both defendants filed this motion in a timely fashion, provide a proposed pleading, and demonstrate excusable neglect and/or mistake. Easy.Ly did not respond to the complaint in a timely fashion because Mr. Roth received the service package after default was entered. Mr. Roth's belief that Aspire Rock, LLC was not properly served was a reasonable mistake of law. The Court denies the requests for sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTs KYLE AGUILAR & WHAM-O, INC. DBA BZ BODYBOARDS' motion To Transfer Case To Los Angeles County Superior Court Or Dismiss. Continued to July 22, 2013 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party shall deliver courtesy copies of its opposition by June 21, 2013 at 4:00 pm to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA PACE VS. TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Toc Ompel Claimant'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Document Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To cOmpel Claimant'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Document Production. Granted, as follows. Within 20 days, Ms. Pace shall serve verified further responses to Form Interrogatories as follows: 2.13, except that she need not identify by name any prescription medications that are both unrelated to any injury she claims and that do not affect one's ability to perceive. If there are such prescription medications that fit this category, Ms. Pace shall indicate so in the response to this interrogatory, without identifying the name of the drug(s) or the physician. If counsel for moving party seeks to inquire further, he shall meet and confer with Ms. Pace's counsel on the terms of a protective order to obtain this information, and the parties may seek further relief if necessary; 6.7; and 12.4. Ms. Pace shall respond fully and completely to each sub-part of each form interrogatory in sequential order, with each response identified by the subpart to which it responds. Ms. Pace shall also serve verified further responses and produce for inspection and copying responsive documents to Requests for Production, nos. 1, 2 and 3. No monetary sanctions are issued. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ABS SEAFOOD, INC., A CORPORATION VS. MAIN STREET MEAT & FISH CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 20, PLAINTIFF ABS SEAFOOD, INC.'s hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Denied, without prejudice. Plaintiff did not file proof of service that this application was served on defendant. No opposition was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the Plaintiff's counsel in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ABS SEAFOOD, INC., A CORPORATION VS. ZHI CHAO WU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 21, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFF ABS SEAFOOD, INC.'s Hearing For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Granted, for good cause shown. A right to attach order shall issue in the amount for $ 7,847.91 that includes the outstanding amount owed of $ 5,847.89, estimated costs of $ 1,000.00, and attorney fees of $ 1,000.00 per Civ. Code § 1717.5(a). An undertaking of $ 10,000.00 is required. No opposition wsa filed. Counsel shall bring a proposed order on the Judicial Council form to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
